queries.query: our spring monolith has entities that are basically getter/setter bags and all the logic lives in fat service classes. can you audit whether our domain is anemic and tell me how bad it is?

i think we have layer violations — our use-case classes import the JPA repository implementations directly and a couple domain entities have @Entity annotations from hibernate. audit the dependency direction across our modules
review our hexagonal setup — i'm not sure our ports and adapters are actually right, feels like framework types are leaking into the core. score how clean the boundaries are
we split billing and catalog into two bounded contexts but they share a giant database table and import each other's models. audit the context seam and tell me where the boundary is broken

transactions and error handling are smeared across every layer in our codebase — controllers open db transactions, the domain throws HTTP-shaped exceptions. audit how cross-cutting concerns flow through the layers
do a full architecture review of this service across dependency direction, boundaries, the domain model, and context seams before our quarterly review
verify whether CA-DEP-002 is fixed using docs/audits/architecture-audit-workflow-state-2026-05-22-billing.json and this PR diff
we suspect business rules have leaked out of our entities into a pile of *Service classes. run a structured pass and score the domain model 0-10
audit our package graph for cycles between top-level modules and any place an outer module is imported by an inner one
we're starting a new go service with two bounded contexts and an unclear seam between them. help me decide where the boundary should go before we write code
our handlers call the database directly. plan a sequenced, reversible refactor to introduce ports and adapters without a big rewrite
the team is new to this codebase — explain ports-and-adapters and the dependency rule against our actual structure, not a textbook
our cli's --help output is dense and new joiners can't tell --region from --zone. take a pass at the help layout and tell me what to tighten
audit the accessibility of our checkout page for WCAG 2.2 AA compliance, here's the page html
our consumer signup form has a 60% drop-off. help me reduce friction on the 4-step form
the production database is hot at 2pm every day on our reporting query, help me trace what's causing it. postgres 14 with pgbouncer
refactor this typescript function to use early returns instead of nested if statements
Claude Code keeps tripping on our repo — wrong test commands, blocked pushes, bad AGENTS.md. make it agent-friendly
